qas.a: versions of a gene that code for a trait and are found on chromosomes
the genetic makeup of a specific set of genes - the letters that code for a trait
the physical characteristics, or traits, of an individual organism - what the organism looks like

qas.q: Gregor Mendel was the "Father of Genetics" --
qas.a: who found that dominant and recessive traits are randomly passed down from parents to offspring
the allele that is expressed when two different alleles or two dominant alleles are present - the overpowering allele - represented by an uppercase letter
the allele that is expressed only when two copies are present - the hidden allele - represented by a lowercase letter
when an organism has two different alleles for their genotype (ex: Tt)
when an organism has two of the same alleles for their genotype (ex: tt)
when the dominant allele is expressed in a heterozygous individual - ex: TT = tall, tt = short, Tt = tall
